name: Valle de la Garganta del Niño Cojo — circular desde Piedralaves
region_intro: Hiking around Piedralaves offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Valle del Tiétar, situated on the southern slopes of the Sierra de Gredos. The region is characterized by a mix of mountains, valleys, and lush vegetation, including pine, chestnut, and oak forests. A prominent feature is the Garganta del Nuño Cojo, a gorge with flowing waters and waterfalls, which many trails follow. This natural setting provides a variety of hiking experiences, from riverside walks to more challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Piedralaves The most popular hiking route is Valle de la Garganta del Niño Cojo — circular desde Piedralaves, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic gorge and valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Fountain – Aigüestosa Viewpoint loop from Piedralaves, a moderate 6.8 miles (10.9 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views from the Aigüestosa Viewpoint and passes by natural springs. Local hikers also love the La Rubiera (2,002 m) – Lanchamala (1,994 m) loop from Embalse de Piedralaves, a 8.7 miles (14.1 km) trail leading through high mountain terrain to peaks like La Rubiera and Lanchamala, often completed in about 6 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Piedralaves is defined by the gorges of the Valle del Tiétar, the forested slopes of the Sierra de Gredos, and trails leading to impressive viewpoints. The network offers options for easy walks, moderate excursions, and difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Piedralaves are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 470 hikers have used komoot to explore Piedralaves's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Piedralaves?
A: Piedralaves offers a diverse selection of over 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including 2 easy walks, 18 moderate excursions, and 3 more challenging mountain ascents, ensuring there's something for everyone to explore the beautiful Valle del Tiétar and Sierra de Gredos.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Piedralaves?
A: Thanks to its location on the southern slopes of the Sierra de Gredos, Piedralaves enjoys a mild climate, making it suitable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Even winter hiking is possible, with the surrounding mountains providing some protection, though higher routes might require more preparation.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Piedralaves?
A: Yes, Piedralaves has options suitable for families. While specific 'easy' routes are available, many of the moderate trails can be adapted for a family outing. Look for routes that follow the lower sections of the Garganta del Nuño Cojo, which offer gentle paths and the refreshing sound of flowing water, perfect for a leisurely walk with children.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Piedralaves?
A: Absolutely. Many of the popular trails in Piedralaves are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144753252/valle-de-la-garganta-del-nino-cojo-circular-desde-piedralaves" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de la Garganta del Niño Cojo — circular desde Piedralaves&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.8-mile (7.7 km) loop exploring the scenic gorge and valley.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Yes, the region is known for its water features. The &lt;em&gt;Garganta del Nuño Cojo&lt;/em&gt; is a primary natural highlight, featuring waterfalls and flowing waters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2923227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa del Horcajo&lt;/a&gt; is another significant landmark at the confluence of several gorges, and the &lt;em&gt;Charca de la Nieta&lt;/em&gt; is a favored natural swimming spot within the gorge, often serving as a starting point for trails.

Q: What are some challenging mountain trails near Piedralaves?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the proximity to the Sierra de Gredos offers more demanding ascents. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17315513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rubiera (2,002 m) – Lanchamala (1,994 m) loop from Embalse de Piedralaves&lt;/a&gt;. This 8.7-mile (14.1 km) trail leads through high mountain terrain to peaks like La Rubiera and Lanchamala, offering significant elevation gain and rewarding views.

Q: Where can I find the best panoramic views on a hike?
A: Many trails in Piedralaves offer exceptional panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21951626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Fountain – Aigüestosa Viewpoint loop from Piedralaves&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.8-mile (10.9 km) path specifically known for its stunning vistas from the Aigüestosa Viewpoint. Higher elevation routes towards the Sierra de Gredos also provide expansive views of the Tiétar Valley.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Piedralaves?
A: The hiking routes in Piedralaves are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the serene gorges and waterfalls to the lush pine, chestnut, and oak forests, as well as the rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2923227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa del Horcajo&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark. You might also encounter charming bridges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mosquea&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4268792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Viejo de Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt;, which are often part of local routes. The area also features several natural pools and reservoirs, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4327965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca de los Cangrejos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any longer, moderate hikes for a full day out?
A: For a longer, moderate full-day hike, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31436053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedralaves – Presa del Horcajo loop from Piedralaves&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers approximately 9.7 miles (15.6 km) and takes around 4 hours 50 minutes, leading you through varied terrain to the significant Presa del Horcajo landmark.

Q: What kind of forests can I expect to see while hiking in Piedralaves?
A: The hiking trails around Piedralaves are often enveloped by diverse and beautiful forests. You can expect to walk through areas dominated by pines, chestnuts, and oaks, which contribute significantly to the scenic beauty and varied terrain of the region, especially along the gorges and valley slopes.
tips: Small dam that collects the waters that come from the elevated parts as is the case of Pico de Lanchamala. Nice area to take a break with the huge walls that rise close to it!

The valley of the La Garganta del Niño Cojo goes from the ridge of the Sierra de Gredos to the same town of Piedralaves, they leave the Presa de Piedralves and in other parts, a magnificent view of the area.

waypoints.name: Valley of the Garganta del Niño Cojo
